translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And on the other side of Jordan, of the Reubenites, and the Gadites, and of the half tribe of Manasseh, with all manner of instruments of war for the battle, an hundred and twenty thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: All these men of war, that could keep rank, came with a perfect heart to Hebron, to make David king over all Israel: and all the rest also of Israel were of one heart to make David king.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And there they were with David three days, eating and drinking: for their brethren had prepared for them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Moreover they that were nigh them, even unto Issachar and Zebulun and Naphtali, brought bread on asses, and on camels, and on mules, and on oxen, and meat, meal, cakes of figs, and bunches of raisins, and wine, and oil, and oxen, and sheep abundantly: for there was joy in Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David consulted with the captains of thousands and hundreds, and with every leader.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David said unto all the congregation of Israel, If it seem good unto you, and that it be of the LORD our God, let us send abroad unto our brethren every where, that are left in all the land of Israel, and with them also to the priests and Levites which are in their cities and suburbs, that they may gather themselves unto us:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And let us bring again the ark of our God to us: for we enquired not at it in the days of Saul.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the congregation said that they would do so: for the thing was right in the eyes of all the people.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So David gathered all Israel together, from Shihor of Egypt even unto the entering of Hemath, to bring the ark of God from Kirjathjearim.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David went up, and all Israel, to Baalah, that is, to Kirjathjearim, which belonged to Judah, to bring up thence the ark of God the LORD, that dwelleth between the cherubims, whose name is called on it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they carried the ark of God in a new cart out of the house of Abinadab: and Uzza and Ahio drave the cart.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David and all Israel played before God with all their might, and with singing, and with harps, and with psalteries, and with timbrels, and with cymbals, and with trumpets.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when they came unto the threshingfloor of Chidon, Uzza put forth his hand to hold the ark; for the oxen stumbled.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the anger of the LORD was kindled against Uzza, and he smote him, because he put his hand to the ark: and there he died before God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David was displeased, because the LORD had made a breach upon Uzza: wherefore that place is called Perezuzza to this day.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David was afraid of God that day, saying, How shall I bring the ark of God home to me?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So David brought not the ark home to himself to the city of David, but carried it aside into the house of Obededom the Gittite.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the ark of God remained with the family of Obededom in his house three months. And the LORD blessed the house of Obededom, and all that he had.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers to David, and timber of cedars, with masons and carpenters, to build him an house.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David perceived that the LORD had confirmed him king over Israel, for his kingdom was lifted up on high, because of his people Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David took more wives at Jerusalem: and David begat more sons and daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now these are the names of his children which he had in Jerusalem; Shammua, and Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Ibhar, and Elishua, and Elpalet,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Nogah, and Nepheg, and Japhia,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Elishama, and Beeliada, and Eliphalet.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when the Philistines heard that David was anointed king over all Israel, all the Philistines went up to seek David. And David heard of it, and went out against them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the Philistines came and spread themselves in the valley of Rephaim.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David enquired of God, saying, Shall I go up against the Philistines? And wilt thou deliver them into mine hand? And the LORD said unto him, Go up; for I will deliver them into thine hand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So they came up to Baalperazim; and David smote them there. Then David said, God hath broken in upon mine enemies by mine hand like the breaking forth of waters: therefore they called the name of that place Baalperazim.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when they had left their gods there, David gave a commandment, and they were burned with fire.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the Philistines yet again spread themselves abroad in the valley.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore David enquired again of God; and God said unto him, Go not up after them; turn away from them, and come upon them over against the mulberry trees.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it shall be, when thou shalt hear a sound of going in the tops of the mulberry trees, that then thou shalt go out to battle: for God is gone forth before thee to smite the host of the Philistines.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: David therefore did as God commanded him: and they smote the host of the Philistines from Gibeon even to Gazer.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the fame of David went out into all lands; and the LORD brought the fear of him upon all nations.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David made him houses in the city of David, and prepared a place for the ark of God, and pitched for it a tent.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then David said, None ought to carry the ark of God but the Levites: for them hath the LORD chosen to carry the ark of God, and to minister unto him for ever.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David gathered all Israel together to Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of the LORD unto his place, which he had prepared for it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David assembled the children of Aaron, and the Levites:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the sons of Kohath; Uriel the chief, and his brethren an hundred and twenty:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the sons of Merari; Asaiah the chief, and his brethren two hundred and twenty:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the sons of Gershom; Joel the chief, and his brethren an hundred and thirty:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the sons of Elizaphan; Shemaiah the chief, and his brethren two hundred:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the sons of Hebron; Eliel the chief, and his brethren fourscore:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the sons of Uzziel; Amminadab the chief, and his brethren an hundred and twelve.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David called for Zadok and Abiathar the priests, and for the Levites, for Uriel, Asaiah, and Joel, Shemaiah, and Eliel, and Amminadab,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And said unto them, Ye are the chief of the fathers of the Levites: sanctify yourselves, both ye and your brethren, that ye may bring up the ark of the LORD God of Israel unto the place that I have prepared for it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For because ye did it not at the first, the LORD our God made a breach upon us, for that we sought him not after the due order.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So the priests and the Levites sanctified themselves to bring up the ark of the LORD God of Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the children of the Levites bare the ark of God upon their shoulders with the staves thereon, as Moses commanded according to the word of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David spake to the chief of the Levites to appoint their brethren to be the singers with instruments of musick, psalteries and harps and cymbals, sounding, by lifting up the voice with joy.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So the Levites appointed Heman the son of Joel; and of his brethren, Asaph the son of Berechiah; and of the sons of Merari their brethren, Ethan the son of Kushaiah;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And with them their brethren of the second degree, Zechariah, Ben, and Jaaziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Unni, Eliab, and Benaiah, and Maaseiah, and Mattithiah, and Elipheleh, and Mikneiah, and Obededom, and Jeiel, the porters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So the singers, Heman, Asaph, and Ethan, were appointed to sound with cymbals of brass;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Zechariah, and Aziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Unni, and Eliab, and Maaseiah, and Benaiah, with psalteries on Alamoth;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Mattithiah, and Elipheleh, and Mikneiah, and Obededom, and Jeiel, and Azaziah, with harps on the Sheminith to excel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Chenaniah, chief of the Levites, was for song: he instructed about the song, because he was skilful.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Berechiah and Elkanah were doorkeepers for the ark.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Shebaniah, and Jehoshaphat, and Nethaneel, and Amasai, and Zechariah, and Benaiah, and Eliezer, the priests, did blow with the trumpets before the ark of God: and Obededom and Jehiah were doorkeepers for the ark.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So David, and the elders of Israel, and the captains over thousands, went to bring up the ark of the covenant of the LORD out of the house of Obededom with joy.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass, when God helped the Levites that bare the ark of the covenant of the LORD, that they offered seven bullocks and seven rams.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David was clothed with a robe of fine linen, and all the Levites that bare the ark, and the singers, and Chenaniah the master of the song with the singers: David also had upon him an ephod of linen.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thus all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the LORD with shouting, and with sound of the cornet, and with trumpets, and with cymbals, making a noise with psalteries and harps.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass, as the ark of the covenant of the LORD came to the city of David, that Michal, the daughter of Saul looking out at a window saw king David dancing and playing: and she despised him in her heart.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So they brought the ark of God, and set it in the midst of the tent that David had pitched for it: and they offered burnt sacrifices and peace offerings before God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when David had made an end of offering the burnt offerings and the peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he dealt to every one of Israel, both man and woman, to every one a loaf of bread, and a good piece of flesh, and a flagon of wine.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he appointed certain of the Levites to minister before the ark of the LORD, and to record, and to thank and praise the LORD God of Israel:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Asaph the chief, and next to him Zechariah, Jeiel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Mattithiah, and Eliab, and Benaiah, and Obededom: and Jeiel with psalteries and with harps; but Asaph made a sound with cymbals;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Benaiah also and Jahaziel the priests with trumpets continually before the ark of the covenant of God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then on that day David delivered first this psalm to thank the LORD into the hand of Asaph and his brethren.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Give thanks unto the LORD, call upon his name, make known his deeds among the people.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Sing unto him, sing psalms unto him, talk ye of all his wondrous works.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Seek the LORD and his strength, seek his face continually.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Remember his marvellous works that he hath done, his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O ye seed of Israel his servant, ye children of Jacob, his chosen ones.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He is the LORD our God; his judgments are in all the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Be ye mindful always of his covenant; the word which he commanded to a thousand generations;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Even of the covenant which he made with Abraham, and of his oath unto Isaac;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And hath confirmed the same to Jacob for a law, and to Israel for an everlasting covenant,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Saying, Unto thee will I give the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When ye were but few, even a few, and strangers in it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when they went from nation to nation, and from one kingdom to another people;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He suffered no man to do them wrong: yea, he reproved kings for their sakes,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Saying, Touch not mine anointed, and do my prophets no harm.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Sing unto the LORD, all the earth; shew forth from day to day his salvation.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Declare his glory among the heathen; his marvellous works among all nations.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised: he also is to be feared above all gods.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For all the gods of the people are idols: but the LORD made the heavens.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Glory and honour are in his presence; strength and gladness are in his place.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Give unto the LORD, ye kindreds of the people, give unto the LORD glory and strength.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Give unto the LORD the glory due unto his name: bring an offering, and come before him: worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Fear before him, all the earth: the world also shall be stable, that it be not moved.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let the heavens be glad, and let the earth rejoice: and let men say among the nations, The LORD reigneth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof: let the fields rejoice, and all that is therein.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then shall the trees of the wood sing out at the presence of the LORD, because he cometh to judge the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good; for his mercy endureth for ever.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And say ye, Save us, O God of our salvation, and gather us together, and deliver us from the heathen, that we may give thanks to thy holy name, and glory in thy praise.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Blessed be the LORD God of Israel for ever and ever. And all the people said, Amen, and praised the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So he left there before the ark of the covenant of the LORD Asaph and his brethren, to minister before the ark continually, as every day’s work required:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Obededom with their brethren, threescore and eight; Obededom also the son of Jeduthun and Hosah to be porters:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Zadok the priest, and his brethren the priests, before the tabernacle of the LORD in the high place that was at Gibeon,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To offer burnt offerings unto the LORD upon the altar of the burnt offering continually morning and evening, and to do according to all that is written in the law of the LORD, which he commanded Israel;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And with them Heman and Jeduthun, and the rest that were chosen, who were expressed by name, to give thanks to the LORD, because his mercy endureth for ever;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And with them Heman and Jeduthun with trumpets and cymbals for those that should make a sound, and with musical instruments of God. And the sons of Jeduthun were porters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the people departed every man to his house: and David returned to bless his house.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now it came to pass, as David sat in his house, that David said to Nathan the prophet, Lo, I dwell in an house of cedars, but the ark of the covenant of the LORD remaineth under curtains.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Nathan said unto David, Do all that is in thine heart; for God is with thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass the same night, that the word of God came to Nathan, saying,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Go and tell David my servant, Thus saith the LORD, Thou shalt not build me an house to dwell in:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For I have not dwelt in an house since the day that I brought up Israel unto this day; but have gone from tent to tent, and from one tabernacle to another.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wheresoever I have walked with all Israel, spake I a word to any of the judges of Israel, whom I commanded to feed my people, saying, Why have ye not built me an house of cedars?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now therefore thus shalt thou say unto my servant David, Thus saith the LORD of hosts, I took thee from the sheepcote, even from following the sheep, that thou shouldest be ruler over my people Israel:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I have been with thee whithersoever thou hast walked, and have cut off all thine enemies from before thee, and have made thee a name like the name of the great men that are in the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also I will ordain a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, and they shall dwell in their place, and shall be moved no more; neither shall the children of wickedness waste them any more, as at the beginning,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And since the time that I commanded judges to be over my people Israel. Moreover I will subdue all thine enemies. Furthermore I tell thee that the LORD will build thee an house.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when thy days be expired that thou must go to be with thy fathers, that I will raise up thy seed after thee, which shall be of thy sons; and I will establish his kingdom.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall build me an house, and I will stablish his throne for ever.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will be his father, and he shall be my son: and I will not take my mercy away from him, as I took it from him that was before thee:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But I will settle him in mine house and in my kingdom for ever: and his throne shall be established for evermore.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so did Nathan speak unto David.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David the king came and sat before the LORD, and said, Who am I, O LORD God, and what is mine house, that thou hast brought me hitherto?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And yet this was a small thing in thine eyes, O God; for thou hast also spoken of thy servant’s house for a great while to come, and hast regarded me according to the estate of a man of high degree, O LORD God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What can David speak more to thee for the honour of thy servant? for thou knowest thy servant.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O LORD, for thy servant’s sake, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all this greatness, in making known all these great things.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O LORD, there is none like thee, neither is there any God beside thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And what one nation in the earth is like thy people Israel, whom God went to redeem to be his own people, to make thee a name of greatness and terribleness, by driving out nations from before thy people, whom thou hast redeemed out of Egypt?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For thy people Israel didst thou make thine own people for ever; and thou, LORD, becamest their God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore now, LORD, let the thing that thou hast spoken concerning thy servant and concerning his house be established for ever, and do as thou hast said.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let it even be established, that thy name may be magnified for ever, saying, The LORD of hosts is the God of Israel, even a God to Israel: and let the house of David thy servant be established before thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For thou, O my God, hast told thy servant that thou wilt build him an house: therefore thy servant hath found in his heart to pray before thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And now, LORD, thou art God, and hast promised this goodness unto thy servant:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now therefore let it please thee to bless the house of thy servant, that it may be before thee for ever: for thou blessest, O LORD, and it shall be blessed for ever.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now after this it came to pass, that David smote the Philistines, and subdued them, and took Gath and her towns out of the hand of the Philistines.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he smote Moab; and the Moabites became David’s servants, and brought gifts.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David smote Hadarezer king of Zobah unto Hamath, as he went to stablish his dominion by the river Euphrates.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David took from him a thousand chariots, and seven thousand horsemen, and twenty thousand footmen: David also houghed all the chariot horses, but reserved of them an hundred chariots.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadarezer king of Zobah, David slew of the Syrians two and twenty thousand men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then David put garrisons in Syriadamascus; and the Syrians became David’s servants, and brought gifts. Thus the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David took the shields of gold that were on the servants of Hadarezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Likewise from Tibhath, and from Chun, cities of Hadarezer, brought David very much brass, wherewith Solomon made the brasen sea, and the pillars, and the vessels of brass.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now when Tou king of Hamath heard how David had smitten all the host of Hadarezer king of Zobah;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He sent Hadoram his son to king David, to enquire of his welfare, and to congratulate him, because he had fought against Hadarezer, and smitten him; (for Hadarezer had war with Tou;) and with him all manner of vessels of gold and silver and brass.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Them also king David dedicated unto the LORD, with the silver and the gold that he brought from all these nations; from Edom, and from Moab, and from the children of Ammon, and from the Philistines, and from Amalek.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Moreover Abishai the son of Zeruiah slew of the Edomites in the valley of salt eighteen thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he put garrisons in Edom; and all the Edomites became David’s servants. Thus the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So David reigned over all Israel, and executed judgment and justice among all his people.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Joab the son of Zeruiah was over the host; and Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud, recorder.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Zadok the son of Ahitub, and Abimelech the son of Abiathar, were the priests; and Shavsha was scribe;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and the sons of David were chief about the king.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now it came to pass after this, that Nahash the king of the children of Ammon died, and his son reigned in his stead.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David said, I will shew kindness unto Hanun the son of Nahash, because his father shewed kindness to me. And David sent messengers to comfort him concerning his father. So the servants of David came into the land of the children of Ammon to Hanun, to comfort him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But the princes of the children of Ammon said to Hanun, Thinkest thou that David doth honour thy father, that he hath sent comforters unto thee? are not his servants come unto thee for to search, and to overthrow, and to spy out the land?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wherefore Hanun took David’s servants, and shaved them, and cut off their garments in the midst hard by their buttocks, and sent them away.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then there went certain, and told David how the men were served. And he sent to meet them: for the men were greatly ashamed. And the king said, Tarry at Jericho until your beards be grown, and then return.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when the children of Ammon saw that they had made themselves odious to David, Hanun and the children of Ammon sent a thousand talents of silver to hire them chariots and horsemen out of Mesopotamia, and out of Syriamaachah, and out of Zobah.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So they hired thirty and two thousand chariots, and the king of Maachah and his people; who came and pitched before Medeba. And the children of Ammon gathered themselves together from their cities, and came to battle.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when David heard of it, he sent Joab, and all the host of the mighty men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the children of Ammon came out, and put the battle in array before the gate of the city: and the kings that were come were by themselves in the field.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now when Joab saw that the battle was set against him before and behind, he chose out of all the choice of Israel, and put them in array against the Syrians.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the rest of the people he delivered unto the hand of Abishai his brother, and they set themselves in array against the children of Ammon.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said, If the Syrians be too strong for me, then thou shalt help me: but if the children of Ammon be too strong for thee, then I will help thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Be of good courage, and let us behave ourselves valiantly for our people, and for the cities of our God: and let the LORD do that which is good in his sight.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So Joab and the people that were with him drew nigh before the Syrians unto the battle; and they fled before him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when the children of Ammon saw that the Syrians were fled, they likewise fled before Abishai his brother, and entered into the city. Then Joab came to Jerusalem.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when the Syrians saw that they were put to the worse before Israel, they sent messengers, and drew forth the Syrians that were beyond the river: and Shophach the captain of the host of Hadarezer went before them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it was told David; and he gathered all Israel, and passed over Jordan, and came upon them, and set the battle in array against them. So when David had put the battle in array against the Syrians, they fought with him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But the Syrians fled before Israel; and David slew of the Syrians seven thousand men which fought in chariots, and forty thousand footmen, and killed Shophach the captain of the host.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when the servants of Hadarezer saw that they were put to the worse before Israel, they made peace with David, and became his servants: neither would the Syrians help the children of Ammon any more.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass, that after the year was expired, at the time that kings go out to battle, Joab led forth the power of the army, and wasted the country of the children of Ammon, and came and besieged Rabbah. But David tarried at Jerusalem. And Joab smote Rabbah, and destroyed it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David took the crown of their king from off his head, and found it to weigh a talent of gold, and there were precious stones in it; and it was set upon David’s head: and he brought also exceeding much spoil out of the city.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he brought out the people that were in it, and cut them with saws, and with harrows of iron, and with axes. Even so dealt David with all the cities of the children of Ammon. And David and all the people returned to Jerusalem.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass after this, that there arose war at Gezer with the Philistines; at which time Sibbechai the Hushathite slew Sippai, that was of the children of the giant: and they were subdued.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And there was war again with the Philistines; and Elhanan the son of Jair slew Lahmi the brother of Goliath the Gittite, whose spear staff was like a weaver’s beam.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And yet again there was war at Gath, where was a man of great stature, whose fingers and toes were four and twenty, six on each hand, and six on each foot and he also was the son of the giant.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But when he defied Israel, Jonathan the son of Shimea David’s brother slew him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These were born unto the giant in Gath; and they fell by the hand of David, and by the hand of his servants.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Satan stood up against Israel, and provoked David to number Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David said to Joab and to the rulers of the people, Go, number Israel from Beersheba even to Dan; and bring the number of them to me, that I may know it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Joab answered, The LORD make his people an hundred times so many more as they be: but, my lord the king, are they not all my lord’s servants? why then doth my lord require this thing? why will he be a cause of trespass to Israel?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Nevertheless the king’s word prevailed against Joab. Wherefore Joab departed, and went throughout all Israel, and came to Jerusalem.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Joab gave the sum of the number of the people unto David. And all they of Israel were a thousand thousand and an hundred thousand men that drew sword: and Judah was four hundred threescore and ten thousand men that drew sword.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But Levi and Benjamin counted he not among them: for the king’s word was abominable to Joab.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God was displeased with this thing; therefore he smote Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David said unto God, I have sinned greatly, because I have done this thing: but now, I beseech thee, do away the iniquity of thy servant; for I have done very foolishly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD spake unto Gad, David’s seer, saying,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Go and tell David, saying, Thus saith the LORD, I offer thee three things: choose thee one of them, that I may do it unto thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So Gad came to David, and said unto him, Thus saith the LORD, Choose thee

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Either three years’ famine; or three months to be destroyed before thy foes, while that the sword of thine enemies overtaketh thee; or else three days the sword of the LORD, even the pestilence, in the land, and the angel of the LORD destroying throughout all the coasts of Israel. Now therefore advise thyself what word I shall bring again to him that sent me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David said unto Gad, I am in a great strait: let me fall now into the hand of the LORD; for very great are his mercies: but let me not fall into the hand of man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So the LORD sent pestilence upon Israel: and there fell of Israel seventy thousand men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God sent an angel unto Jerusalem to destroy it: and as he was destroying, the LORD beheld, and he repented him of the evil, and said to the angel that destroyed, It is enough, stay now thine hand. And the angel of the LORD stood by the threshingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David lifted up his eyes, and saw the angel of the LORD stand between the earth and the heaven, having a drawn sword in his hand stretched out over Jerusalem. Then David and the elders of Israel, who were clothed in sackcloth, fell upon their faces.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David said unto God, Is it not I that commanded the people to be numbered? even I it is that have sinned and done evil indeed; but as for these sheep, what have they done? let thine hand, I pray thee, O LORD my God, be on me, and on my father’s house; but not on thy people, that they should be plagued.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then the angel of the LORD commanded Gad to say to David, that David should go up, and set up an altar unto the LORD in the threshingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David went up at the saying of Gad, which he spake in the name of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Ornan turned back, and saw the angel; and his four sons with him hid themselves. Now Ornan was threshing wheat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And as David came to Ornan, Ornan looked and saw David, and went out of the threshingfloor, and bowed himself to David with his face to the ground.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then David said to Ornan, Grant me the place of this threshingfloor, that I may build an altar therein unto the LORD: thou shalt grant it me for the full price: that the plague may be stayed from the people.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Ornan said unto David, Take it to thee, and let my lord the king do that which is good in his eyes: lo, I give thee the oxen also for burnt offerings, and the threshing instruments for wood, and the wheat for the meat offering; I give it all.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And king David said to Ornan, Nay; but I will verily buy it for the full price: for I will not take that which is thine for the LORD, nor offer burnt offerings without cost.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So David gave to Ornan for the place six hundred shekels of gold by weight.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David built there an altar unto the LORD, and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings, and called upon the LORD; and he answered him from heaven by fire upon the altar of burnt offering.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD commanded the angel; and he put up his sword again into the sheath thereof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: At that time when David saw that the LORD had answered him in the threshingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite, then he sacrificed there.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For the tabernacle of the LORD, which Moses made in the wilderness, and the altar of the burnt offering, were at that season in the high place at Gibeon.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But David could not go before it to enquire of God: for he was afraid because of the sword of the angel of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then David said, This is the house of the LORD God, and this is the altar of the burnt offering for Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David commanded to gather together the strangers that were in the land of Israel; and he set masons to hew wrought stones to build the house of God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David prepared iron in abundance for the nails for the doors of the gates, and for the joinings; and brass in abundance without weight;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also cedar trees in abundance: for the Zidonians and they of Tyre brought much cedar wood to David.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David said, Solomon my son is young and tender, and the house that is to be builded for the LORD must be exceeding magnifical, of fame and of glory throughout all countries: I will therefore now make preparation for it. So David prepared abundantly before his death.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then he called for Solomon his son, and charged him to build an house for the LORD God of Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David said to Solomon, My son, as for me, it was in my mind to build an house unto the name of the LORD my God:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But the word of the LORD came to me, saying, Thou hast shed blood abundantly, and hast made great wars: thou shalt not build an house unto my name, because thou hast shed much blood upon the earth in my sight.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, a son shall be born to thee, who shall be a man of rest; and I will give him rest from all his enemies round about: for his name shall be Solomon, and I will give peace and quietness unto Israel in his days.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall build an house for my name; and he shall be my son, and I will be his father; and I will establish the throne of his kingdom over Israel for ever.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now, my son, the LORD be with thee; and prosper thou, and build the house of the LORD thy God, as he hath said of thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Only the LORD give thee wisdom and understanding, and give thee charge concerning Israel, that thou mayest keep the law of the LORD thy God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then shalt thou prosper, if thou takest heed to fulfil the statutes and judgments which the LORD charged Moses with concerning Israel: be strong, and of good courage; dread not, nor be dismayed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now, behold, in my trouble I have prepared for the house of the LORD an hundred thousand talents of gold, and a thousand thousand talents of silver; and of brass and iron without weight; for it is in abundance: timber also and stone have I prepared; and thou mayest add thereto.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Moreover there are workmen with thee in abundance, hewers and workers of stone and timber, and all manner of cunning men for every manner of work.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the gold, the silver, and the brass, and the iron, there is no number. Arise therefore, and be doing, and the LORD be with thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: David also commanded all the princes of Israel to help Solomon his son, saying,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is not the LORD your God with you? and hath he not given you rest on every side? for he hath given the inhabitants of the land into mine hand; and the land is subdued before the LORD, and before his people.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now set your heart and your soul to seek the LORD your God; arise therefore, and build ye the sanctuary of the LORD God, to bring the ark of the covenant of the LORD, and the holy vessels of God, into the house that is to be built to the name of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So when David was old and full of days, he made Solomon his son king over Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he gathered together all the princes of Israel, with the priests and the Levites.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now the Levites were numbered from the age of thirty years and upward: and their number by their polls, man by man, was thirty and eight thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of which, twenty and four thousand were to set forward the work of the house of the LORD; and six thousand were officers and judges:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Moreover four thousand were porters; and four thousand praised the LORD with the instruments which I made, said David, to praise therewith.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David divided them into courses among the sons of Levi, namely, Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the Gershonites were, Laadan, and Shimei.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons of Laadan; the chief was Jehiel, and Zetham, and Joel, three.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons of Shimei; Shelomith, and Haziel, and Haran, three. These were the chief of the fathers of Laadan.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the sons of Shimei were, Jahath, Zina, and Jeush, and Beriah. These four were the sons of Shimei.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Jahath was the chief, and Zizah the second: but Jeush and Beriah had not many sons; therefore they were in one reckoning, according to their father’s house.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons of Kohath; Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel, four.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons of Amram; Aaron and Moses: and Aaron was separated, that he should sanctify the most holy things, he and his sons for ever, to burn incense before the LORD, to minister unto him, and to bless in his name for ever.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now concerning Moses the man of God, his sons were named of the tribe of Levi.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons of Moses were, Gershom, and Eliezer.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the sons of Gershom, Shebuel was the chief.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the sons of Eliezer were, Rehabiah the chief. And Eliezer had none other sons; but the sons of Rehabiah were very many.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the sons of Izhar; Shelomith the chief.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the sons of Hebron; Jeriah the first, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the sons of Uzziel; Micah the first, and Jesiah the second.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons of Merari; Mahli, and Mushi. The sons of Mahli; Eleazar, and Kish.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Eleazar died, and had no sons, but daughters: and their brethren the sons of Kish took them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons of Mushi; Mahli, and Eder, and Jeremoth, three.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These were the sons of Levi after the house of their fathers; even the chief of the fathers, as they were counted by number of names by their polls, that did the work for the service of the house of the LORD, from the age of twenty years and upward.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For David said, The LORD God of Israel hath given rest unto his people, that they may dwell in Jerusalem for ever:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And also unto the Levites; they shall no more carry the tabernacle, nor any vessels of it for the service thereof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For by the last words of David the Levites were numbered from twenty years old and above:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because their office was to wait on the sons of Aaron for the service of the house of the LORD, in the courts, and in the chambers, and in the purifying of all holy things, and the work of the service of the house of God;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Both for the shewbread, and for the fine flour for meat offering, and for the unleavened cakes, and for that which is baked in the pan, and for that which is fried, and for all manner of measure and size;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And to stand every morning to thank and praise the LORD, and likewise at even;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And to offer all burnt sacrifices unto the LORD in the sabbaths, in the new moons, and on the set feasts, by number, according to the order commanded unto them, continually before the LORD:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And that they should keep the charge of the tabernacle of the congregation, and the charge of the holy place, and the charge of the sons of Aaron their brethren, in the service of the house of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now these are the divisions of the sons of Aaron. The sons of Aaron; Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But Nadab and Abihu died before their father, and had no children: therefore Eleazar and Ithamar executed the priest’s office.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David distributed them, both Zadok of the sons of Eleazar, and Ahimelech of the sons of Ithamar, according to their offices in their service.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And there were more chief men found of the sons of Eleazar than of the sons of Ithamar; and thus were they divided. Among the sons of Eleazar there were sixteen chief men of the house of their fathers, and eight among the sons of Ithamar according to the house of their fathers.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thus were they divided by lot, one sort with another; for the governors of the sanctuary, and governors of the house of God, were of the sons of Eleazar, and of the sons of Ithamar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Shemaiah the son of Nethaneel the scribe, one of the Levites, wrote them before the king, and the princes, and Zadok the priest, and Ahimelech the son of Abiathar, and before the chief of the fathers of the priests and Levites: one principal household being taken for Eleazar, and one taken for Ithamar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now the first lot came forth to Jehoiarib, the second to Jedaiah,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The third to Harim, the fourth to Seorim,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The fifth to Malchijah, the sixth to Mijamin,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The seventh to Hakkoz, the eighth to Abijah,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The ninth to Jeshuah, the tenth to Shecaniah,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The eleventh to Eliashib, the twelfth to Jakim,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The thirteenth to Huppah, the fourteenth to Jeshebeab,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The fifteenth to Bilgah, the sixteenth to Immer,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The seventeenth to Hezir, the eighteenth to Aphses,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The nineteenth to Pethahiah, the twentieth to Jehezekel,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The one and twentieth to Jachin, the two and twentieth to Gamul,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The three and twentieth to Delaiah, the four and twentieth to Maaziah.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These were the orderings of them in their service to come into the house of the LORD, according to their manner, under Aaron their father, as the LORD God of Israel had commanded him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the rest of the sons of Levi were these: Of the sons of Amram; Shubael: of the sons of Shubael; Jehdeiah.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Concerning Rehabiah: of the sons of Rehabiah, the first was Isshiah.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the Izharites; Shelomoth: of the sons of Shelomoth; Jahath.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the sons of Hebron; Jeriah the first, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, Jekameam the fourth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the sons of Uzziel; Michah: of the sons of Michah; Shamir.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The brother of Michah was Isshiah: of the sons of Isshiah; Zechariah.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons of Merari were Mahli and Mushi: the sons of Jaaziah; Beno.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons of Merari by Jaaziah; Beno, and Shoham, and Zaccur, and Ibri.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of Mahli came Eleazar, who had no sons.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Concerning Kish: the son of Kish was Jerahmeel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons also of Mushi; Mahli, and Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the sons of the Levites after the house of their fathers.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These likewise cast lots over against their brethren the sons of Aaron in the presence of David the king, and Zadok, and Ahimelech, and the chief of the fathers of the priests and Levites, even the principal fathers over against their younger brethren.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Moreover David and the captains of the host separated to the service of the sons of Asaph, and of Heman, and of Jeduthun, who should prophesy with harps, with psalteries, and with cymbals: and the number of the workmen according to their service was:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the sons of Asaph; Zaccur, and Joseph, and Nethaniah, and Asarelah, the sons of Asaph under the hands of Asaph, which prophesied according to the order of the king.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of Jeduthun: the sons of Jeduthun; Gedaliah, and Zeri, and Jeshaiah, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah, six, under the hands of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with a harp, to give thanks and to praise the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of Heman: the sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, and Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, and Romamtiezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: All these were the sons of Heman the king’s seer in the words of God, to lift up the horn. And God gave to Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: All these were under the hands of their father for song in the house of the LORD, with cymbals, psalteries, and harps, for the service of the house of God, according to the king’s order to Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So the number of them, with their brethren that were instructed in the songs of the LORD, even all that were cunning, was two hundred fourscore and eight.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they cast lots, ward against ward, as well the small as the great, the teacher as the scholar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now the first lot came forth for Asaph to Joseph: the second to Gedaliah, who with his brethren and sons were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The third to Zaccur, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The fourth to Izri, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The fifth to Nethaniah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sixth to Bukkiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The seventh to Jesharelah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The eighth to Jeshaiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The ninth to Mattaniah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The tenth to Shimei, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The eleventh to Azareel, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The twelfth to Hashabiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The thirteenth to Shubael, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The fourteenth to Mattithiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The fifteenth to Jeremoth, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sixteenth to Hananiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The seventeenth to Joshbekashah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The eighteenth to Hanani, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The nineteenth to Mallothi, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The twentieth to Eliathah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The one and twentieth to Hothir, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The two and twentieth to Giddalti, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The three and twentieth to Mahazioth, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The four and twentieth to Romamtiezer, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Concerning the divisions of the porters: Of the Korhites was Meshelemiah the son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the sons of Meshelemiah were, Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Elioenai the seventh.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Moreover the sons of Obededom were, Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, and Sacar the fourth, and Nethaneel the fifth,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peulthai the eighth: for God blessed him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also unto Shemaiah his son were sons born, that ruled throughout the house of their father: for they were mighty men of valour.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons of Shemaiah; Othni, and Rephael, and Obed, Elzabad, whose brethren were strong men, Elihu, and Semachiah.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: All these of the sons of Obededom: they and their sons and their brethren, able men for strength for the service, were threescore and two of Obededom.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Meshelemiah had sons and brethren, strong men, eighteen.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also Hosah, of the children of Merari, had sons; Simri the chief, (for though he was not the firstborn, yet his father made him the chief;)

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth: all the sons and brethren of Hosah were thirteen.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Among these were the divisions of the porters, even among the chief men, having wards one against another, to minister in the house of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they cast lots, as well the small as the great, according to the house of their fathers, for every gate.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the lot eastward fell to Shelemiah. Then for Zechariah his son, a wise counsellor, they cast lots; and his lot came out northward.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To Obededom southward; and to his sons the house of Asuppim.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To Shuppim and Hosah the lot came forth westward, with the gate Shallecheth, by the causeway of the going up, ward against ward.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Eastward were six Levites, northward four a day, southward four a day, and toward Asuppim two and two.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: At Parbar westward, four at the causeway, and two at Parbar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These are the divisions of the porters among the sons of Kore, and among the sons of Merari.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And of the Levites, Ahijah was over the treasures of the house of God, and over the treasures of the dedicated things.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As concerning the sons of Laadan; the sons of the Gershonite Laadan, chief fathers, even of Laadan the Gershonite, were Jehieli.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons of Jehieli; Zetham, and Joel his brother, which were over the treasures of the house of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the Amramites, and the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Shebuel the son of Gershom, the son of Moses, was ruler of the treasures.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And his brethren by Eliezer; Rehabiah his son, and Jeshaiah his son, and Joram his son, and Zichri his son, and Shelomith his son.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which Shelomith and his brethren were over all the treasures of the dedicated things, which David the king, and the chief fathers, the captains over thousands and hundreds, and the captains of the host, had dedicated.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Out of the spoils won in battles did they dedicate to maintain the house of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all that Samuel the seer, and Saul the son of Kish, and Abner the son of Ner, and Joab the son of Zeruiah, had dedicated; and whosoever had dedicated any thing, it was under the hand of Shelomith, and of his brethren.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons were for the outward business over Israel, for officers and judges.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his brethren, men of valour, a thousand and seven hundred, were officers among them of Israel on this side Jordan westward in all the business of the LORD, and in the service of the king.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Among the Hebronites was Jerijah the chief, even among the Hebronites, according to the generations of his fathers. In the fortieth year of the reign of David they were sought for, and there were found among them mighty men of valour at Jazer of Gilead.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And his brethren, men of valour, were two thousand and seven hundred chief fathers, whom king David made rulers over the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half tribe of Manasseh, for every matter pertaining to God, and affairs of the king.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now the children of Israel after their number, to wit, the chief fathers and captains of thousands and hundreds, and their officers that served the king in any matter of the courses, which came in and went out month by month throughout all the months of the year, of every course were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Over the first course for the first month was Jashobeam the son of Zabdiel: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the children of Perez was the chief of all the captains of the host for the first month.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And over the course of the second month was Dodai an Ahohite, and of his course was Mikloth also the ruler: in his course likewise were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The third captain of the host for the third month was Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, a chief priest: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: This is that Benaiah, who was mighty among the thirty, and above the thirty: and in his course was Ammizabad his son.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The fourth captain for the fourth month was Asahel the brother of Joab, and Zebadiah his son after him: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The fifth captain for the fifth month was Shamhuth the Izrahite: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sixth captain for the sixth month was Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The seventh captain for the seventh month was Helez the Pelonite, of the children of Ephraim: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The eighth captain for the eighth month was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zarhites: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The ninth captain for the ninth month was Abiezer the Anetothite, of the Benjamites: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The tenth captain for the tenth month was Maharai the Netophathite, of the Zarhites: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The eleventh captain for the eleventh month was Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the children of Ephraim: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The twelfth captain for the twelfth month was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Furthermore over the tribes of Israel: the ruler of the Reubenites was Eliezer the son of Zichri: of the Simeonites, Shephatiah the son of Maachah:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the Levites, Hashabiah the son of Kemuel: of the Aaronites, Zadok:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of Judah, Elihu, one of the brethren of David: of Issachar, Omri the son of Michael:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of Zebulun, Ishmaiah the son of Obadiah: of Naphtali, Jerimoth the son of Azriel:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the children of Ephraim, Hoshea the son of Azaziah: of the half tribe of Manasseh, Joel the son of Pedaiah:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of the half tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Iddo the son of Zechariah: of Benjamin, Jaasiel the son of Abner:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of Dan, Azareel the son of Jeroham. These were the princes of the tribes of Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But David took not the number of them from twenty years old and under: because the LORD had said he would increase Israel like to the stars of the heavens.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Joab the son of Zeruiah began to number, but he finished not, because there fell wrath for it against Israel; neither was the number put in the account of the chronicles of king David.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And over the king’s treasures was Azmaveth the son of Adiel: and over the storehouses in the fields, in the cities, and in the villages, and in the castles, was Jehonathan the son of Uzziah:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And over them that did the work of the field for tillage of the ground was Ezri the son of Chelub:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And over the vineyards was Shimei the Ramathite: over the increase of the vineyards for the wine cellars was Zabdi the Shiphmite:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And over the olive trees and the sycomore trees that were in the low plains was Baalhanan the Gederite: and over the cellars of oil was Joash:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And over the herds that fed in Sharon was Shitrai the Sharonite: and over the herds that were in the valleys was Shaphat the son of Adlai:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Over the camels also was Obil the Ishmaelite: and over the asses was Jehdeiah the Meronothite:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And over the flocks was Jaziz the Hagerite. All these were the rulers of the substance which was king David’s.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also Jonathan David’s uncle was a counsellor, a wise man, and a scribe: and Jehiel the son of Hachmoni was with the king’s sons:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Ahithophel was the king’s counsellor: and Hushai the Archite was the king’s companion:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And after Ahithophel was Jehoiada the son of Benaiah, and Abiathar: and the general of the king’s army was Joab.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David assembled all the princes of Israel, the princes of the tribes, and the captains of the companies that ministered to the king by course, and the captains over the thousands, and captains over the hundreds, and the stewards over all the substance and possession of the king, and of his sons, with the officers, and with the mighty men, and with all the valiant men, unto Jerusalem.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then David the king stood up upon his feet, and said, Hear me, my brethren, and my people: As for me, I had in mine heart to build an house of rest for the ark of the covenant of the LORD, and for the footstool of our God, and had made ready for the building:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But God said unto me, Thou shalt not build an house for my name, because thou hast been a man of war, and hast shed blood.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Howbeit the LORD God of Israel chose me before all the house of my father to be king over Israel for ever: for he hath chosen Judah to be the ruler; and of the house of Judah, the house of my father; and among the sons of my father he liked me to make me king over all Israel:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And of all my sons, (for the LORD hath given me many sons,) he hath chosen Solomon my son to sit upon the throne of the kingdom of the LORD over Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said unto me, Solomon thy son, he shall build my house and my courts: for I have chosen him to be my son, and I will be his father.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Moreover I will establish his kingdom for ever, if he be constant to do my commandments and my judgments, as at this day.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now therefore in the sight of all Israel the congregation of the LORD, and in the audience of our God, keep and seek for all the commandments of the LORD your God: that ye may possess this good land, and leave it for an inheritance for your children after you for ever.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And thou, Solomon my son, know thou the God of thy father, and serve him with a perfect heart and with a willing mind: for the LORD searcheth all hearts, and understandeth all the imaginations of the thoughts: if thou seek him, he will be found of thee; but if thou forsake him, he will cast thee off for ever.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Take heed now; for the LORD hath chosen thee to build an house for the sanctuary: be strong, and do it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then David gave to Solomon his son the pattern of the porch, and of the houses thereof, and of the treasuries thereof, and of the upper chambers thereof, and of the inner parlours thereof, and of the place of the mercy seat,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the pattern of all that he had by the spirit, of the courts of the house of the LORD, and of all the chambers round about, of the treasuries of the house of God, and of the treasuries of the dedicated things:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also for the courses of the priests and the Levites, and for all the work of the service of the house of the LORD, and for all the vessels of service in the house of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He gave of gold by weight for things of gold, for all instruments of all manner of service; silver also for all instruments of silver by weight, for all instruments of every kind of service:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Even the weight for the candlesticks of gold, and for their lamps of gold, by weight for every candlestick, and for the lamps thereof: and for the candlesticks of silver by weight, both for the candlestick, and also for the lamps thereof, according to the use of every candlestick.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And by weight he gave gold for the tables of shewbread, for every table; and likewise silver for the tables of silver:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also pure gold for the fleshhooks, and the bowls, and the cups: and for the golden basons he gave gold by weight for every bason; and likewise silver by weight for every bason of silver:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And for the altar of incense refined gold by weight; and gold for the pattern of the chariot of the cherubims, that spread out their wings, and covered the ark of the covenant of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: All this, said David, the LORD made me understand in writing by his hand upon me, even all the works of this pattern.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And David said to Solomon his son, Be strong and of good courage, and do it: fear not, nor be dismayed: for the LORD God, even my God, will be with thee; he will not fail thee, nor forsake thee, until thou hast finished all the work for the service of the house of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And, behold, the courses of the priests and the Levites, even they shall be with thee for all the service of the house of God: and there shall be with thee for all manner of workmanship every willing skilful man, for any manner of service: also the princes and all the people will be wholly at thy commandment.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Furthermore David the king said unto all the congregation, Solomon my son, whom alone God hath chosen, is yet young and tender, and the work is great: for the palace is not for man, but for the LORD God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now I have prepared with all my might for the house of my God the gold for things to be made of gold, and the silver for things of silver, and the brass for things of brass, the iron for things of iron, and wood for things of wood; onyx stones, and stones to be set, glistering stones, and of divers colours, and all manner of precious stones, and marble stones in abundance.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Moreover, because I have set my affection to the house of my God, I have of mine own proper good, of gold and silver, which I have given to the house of my God, over and above all that I have prepared for the holy house,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Even three thousand talents of gold, of the gold of Ophir, and seven thousand talents of refined silver, to overlay the walls of the houses withal:

translations.name: King James Version
